microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's resilience validation program into a **platformized architecture**, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual drills and delivering **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise partners including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining self-service scheduling requirements that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned drill execution to a scalable, repeatable model across cloud infrastructure.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, delivering a full drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate** and surfacing critical hardware defects across distributed cloud systems at scale.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a **sovereign-cloud network isolation test** tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract requiring deep cloud architecture expertise.

Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — directly scaling team output through agentic automation on Azure infrastructure.

Validated partner integrations across Databricks, SAP, and NetApp environments, consulting on **distributed data architectures** and ensuring recovery workflows aligned with each partner's cloud-native platform dependencies.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of **AI-driven code generation** workflows, running technical demos and training sessions across 14 Azure service teams that drove sustained utilization and saved **37 engineering hours** monthly.

Shaped the product roadmap to include **intent-based YAML generation** by conducting 11+ structured user interviews with Azure engineering teams, surfacing critical feature gaps in data pipeline authoring workflows.

Improved data retrieval across technical documentation by migrating to an **AI-powered semantic search** system with rigorous metadata standards, cutting average lookup time by 83% for distributed engineering teams.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework, enabling 140+ teams to launch Azure regions 28% faster through standardized distributed workflows.

Drove discovery with 20+ Azure service teams to quantify **81 hours of manual toil** per region launch, then built a roadmap to automate critical data pipeline and operational paths — directly informing the platform's automation strategy.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, giving leadership real-time visibility to target automation gaps and prioritize high-impact investments in cloud infrastructure scaling.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot by defining the OS migration strategy and mapping software dependencies across 1,200+ stations in a large-scale distributed robotics environment.

Facilitated Agile sprint ceremonies and backlog prioritization, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and unblock engineering delivery across cross-functional hardware and software teams.

Drove alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing a repeatable integration model for future fleet rollouts.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, implementing a structured CRM process that standardized the customer acquisition workflow.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a data-driven digital GTM strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile based on channel performance analytics.

Improved leadership pricing models by analyzing **1,000+ project records** to surface margin leaks, delivering a profitability framework that informed go-forward pricing decisions across service lines.
